title: Email sync not fetching today's emails
description: Email sync is only fetching emails up to 3 days old, but not today's emails. Need to investigate the sync logic to find what date/time boundary is causing emails from the past 3 days to be excluded.
notes: Root cause: history.list() in sync_gmail() is missing pagination. The Gmail API returns paginated history results (default 100 records/page). The code only fetches page 1, which contains the oldest events from ~3 days ago. Today's emails are on page 2+ which are never fetched. Fix: add nextPageToken loop to exhaust all pages before processing.
close_reason: Fixed missing pagination in history.list() call. Added nextPageToken loop to exhaust all history pages before processing.

title: Replace Ollama with Anthropic API for agent chat
description:
The current chatbot uses a local Ollama instance (qwen2.5:14b via OpenAI-compatible API) in backend/services/agent.py. Replace this with the Anthropic SDK (claude-sonnet-4-6) so the assistant runs on Claude instead of a local model.

What needs to change:
- Add anthropic Python package to backend dependencies
- Rewrite the tool-use loop in backend/services/agent.py to use the Anthropic SDK with native tool_use content blocks
- Map the existing tools (search_emails, get_email_body, list_recent_emails, list_calendar_events, create_calendar_event, delete_calendar_event) to Anthropic tool schemas
- Keep SSE streaming intact — stream text deltas as they arrive using stream_text_deltas or similar
- Read ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from .env / config.py
- Remove OLLAMA_BASE_URL and OLLAMA_MODEL from required config (or make them optional)
close_reason: Replaced Ollama/OpenAI with Anthropic SDK in agent.py. Uses claude-haiku-4-5-20251001, native tool_use blocks, SSE streaming via stream.text_stream, and prompt caching on system+tools.

title: Gmail incremental sync ignores labelsAdded/labelsRemoved history events
description: The history-based incremental sync in sync_gmail() only processes messagesAdded records. Gmail History API also emits labelsAdded and labelsRemoved events for read/unread and label changes on existing messages — these are silently dropped, so state diverges from Gmail over time.
close_reason: Fixed in gmail_sync.py: added _update_email_labels helper and extended history sync to process labelsAdded/labelsRemoved events

title: Gmail sync skips existing emails — read/unread and labels never update
description: After an email is stored once, _fetch_and_store_messages skips it entirely (if existing: continue). This means is_read, is_starred, and labels are frozen at first-fetch state and never updated when you read, star, or re-label emails in Gmail.
close_reason: Fixed in gmail_sync.py: added _update_email_labels helper and extended history sync to process labelsAdded/labelsRemoved events

title: Easy start and shutdown of the email agent
description: Starting and stopping the app requires manual steps (running start.sh, killing processes). Add a convenient way to start and stop the full stack (backend + frontend) with a single command or script, ideally with clean shutdown handling so ports are not left occupied.
close_reason: Added stop.sh with PID-file tracking. Improved start.sh: checks if already running, uses fuser for reliable port cleanup, writes .pids on startup, cleans up on Ctrl+C.

title: Frontend: Reply composer in email detail view
description: Add a reply UI at the bottom of EmailDetail.tsx. Show a collapsed 'Reply' button that expands into a composer with: pre-filled To (original sender), quoted body, subject prefixed with Re:, and a Send button. On send, call POST /api/emails/{id}/reply. Show a sending spinner and success/error toast. Collapse composer on success.
acceptance_criteria: Reply button visible; composer pre-fills fields; send calls API; success message shown; composer collapses after send

title: Backend: Send/reply REST API and agent tool
description: Expose a unified send/reply API endpoint and add an agent tool. Add POST /api/emails/send (new email) and POST /api/emails/{id}/reply (reply to thread) in backend/routers/emails.py, dispatching to the correct provider service by account. Add draft_and_send_reply and send_email agent tools in backend/services/agent.py so the AI can compose and send on the user's behalf after confirmation.
acceptance_criteria: REST endpoint dispatches to correct provider; agent can draft a reply and send it; endpoint validates account ownership

title: Backend: Gmail send and reply service
description: Implement send and reply via the Gmail API. Add a send_email(account_id, to, subject, body, thread_id=None) function in backend/services/gmail_sync.py (or a new gmail_send.py). Use the Gmail API messages.send endpoint with RFC 2822 message encoding. Reply by including In-Reply-To and References headers and the threadId parameter. Return the sent message ID.
acceptance_criteria: Can send a new email from a Gmail account; can reply to an existing thread; sent message appears in Gmail Sent folder

title: Frontend: Label filter and sort UI
description: Add label-based filtering to the email list. Currently EmailList.tsx shows all emails with no label filter. Need: a label list in the sidebar (Sidebar.tsx) showing all unique labels from loaded emails, clicking a label filters EmailList to show only emails with that label. Also add a sort control (by date, sender, subject). Labels come from GET /api/emails which already returns the labels array.
acceptance_criteria: Sidebar shows all labels; clicking a label filters email list; sort control changes order; selected label is visually highlighted

title: Backend: Agent tool for labeling emails
description: Add a label_email tool to the agent tool loop in backend/services/agent.py so the AI can apply or remove labels via natural language. Should call the same service layer as the REST API. Tool signature: label_email(email_id, labels_to_add, labels_to_remove). Must handle both Gmail label names and generic string labels.
acceptance_criteria: Agent can apply and remove labels when asked; tool is listed in tool definitions; round-trips verified against real emails

title: Backend: Label management API
description: Add REST endpoints to apply and remove labels on emails, and persist changes back to the provider. The Email model has a labels field (JSON list) populated on sync, but there is no write path. Need: PATCH /api/emails/{id}/labels to add/remove labels, update the local DB, and call the Gmail labels.modify API (or IMAP STORE flags for IMAP accounts). Outlook can use categories API.
acceptance_criteria: PATCH endpoint adds/removes labels in DB; Gmail labels sync back to Gmail; IMAP flags updated for IMAP accounts
close_reason: PATCH /api/emails/{id}/labels endpoint added; Gmail labels.modify integration with scope upgrade to gmail.modify; IMAP STORE flag sync; is_read/is_starred kept in sync; updateLabels added to frontend api.ts

title: Epic: Email responding (compose and reply)
description: Allow users and the AI agent to send emails and reply to threads. Currently the app is entirely read-only — no send capability exists anywhere in the stack. This epic covers provider-specific send APIs (Gmail, Outlook, IMAP/SMTP), an agent draft/send tool, and a frontend compose/reply UI.

title: Epic: Email labeling and sorting
description: Enable users and the AI agent to organize emails by applying, removing, and filtering labels. Labels already exist in the DB as a JSON field and are read from Gmail during sync, but there is no way to write labels back to providers or filter by them in the UI. This epic covers the full label workflow.

title: Frontend: Compose new email UI
description: Add a compose button (pencil icon) to the email list header or sidebar in App.tsx / Sidebar.tsx that opens a modal composer. Fields: To, Subject, Body (textarea), From account selector (if multiple accounts configured). On send, call POST /api/emails/send. Show progress and feedback. The modal should be dismissable with a draft warning if body is non-empty.
acceptance_criteria: Compose button opens modal; all required fields present; account selector shows configured accounts; send calls API; modal closes on success with confirmation

title: Backend: IMAP/SMTP send and reply service
description: Implement send/reply for IMAP accounts using SMTP (aiosmtplib). IMAP accounts in the DB have credentials for reading but SMTP send requires separate host/port/credentials. Extend the IMAP account config (Account model or .env) to store SMTP settings. Add send_email and reply_to_email in backend/services/imap_sync.py. Copy sent message to the Sent folder via IMAP APPEND.
acceptance_criteria: Can send email via SMTP for an IMAP account; reply sets correct headers; sent message stored in Sent folder

title: Backend: Outlook send and reply service
description: Implement send and reply via the Microsoft Graph API for Outlook accounts. Add send_email and reply_to_email to backend/services/outlook_sync.py using the /me/sendMail and /me/messages/{id}/reply Graph endpoints with MSAL token refresh. Match the same function signature as the Gmail service for consistency.
acceptance_criteria: Can send a new email from an Outlook account; can reply to a thread; message appears in Outlook Sent Items

title: Frontend: Label editor in email detail view
description: Allow users to add and remove labels directly from the EmailDetail.tsx view. Need: a label chip list showing current labels with remove (x) buttons, a text input or dropdown to add a new label, calling PATCH /api/emails/{id}/labels. Label changes should update the email list in real time (refetch or optimistic update).
acceptance_criteria: User can add a label; user can remove a label; changes persist after page reload; email list reflects label changes
